creator: truckman@FreeBSD.org
text:
Building the port and attempting to install it fails because of several
files that are listed in pkg-plist are not installed by the port.

===>  License ART10 GPLv1 accepted by the user
===>   p5-Inline-0.53 depends on file: /usr/local/sbin/pkg - found
===> Fetching all distfiles required by p5-Inline-0.53 for building

===>   p5-Inline-0.53 depends on file: /usr/local/bin/perl5.16.3 - found
===>  Configuring for p5-Inline-0.53
Checking if your kit is complete...
Looks good

Inline::C is packaged with Inline.pm because it is the most commonly used
Inline Language Support Module (ILSM).

See also: Inline::ASM, ::Awk, ::BC, ::Basic, ::Befunge, ::CPP (C++), ::CPR,
          ::Foo, ::Guile, ::Java, ::Octave, ::PERL, ::Python, ::Ruby, ::TT,
          ::Tcl and ::WebChat.

Config.pm indicates that your version of Perl was built with this C compiler:

    cc

I have located this compiler on your system.

Stop in /usr/ports/devel/p5-Inline.

Fix: 

I was able to work around this problem by removing the missing
	files from pkg-plist.

	I was not able to figure out why all the .pod files were not
	installed or why the missing man pages were not built.
How-To-Repeat: 
	Attempt to build and install the devel/p5-Inline on a machine that
	uses pkgng.
